title: Go/no-go for regulated market entry
description: Your healthtech platform wants to enter a new regulated market. Make the go/no-go recommendation with compliance, cost, and timeline trade-offs.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at MediLedger, a Series C healthtech company (400 employees, $43.3M ARR). Your immediate assignment is go/no-go for regulated market entry. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Optimize for repeatable outcomes over one-off wins that do not scale.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 37179
New logo adds: 2093
Activation rate: 61%
30-day retention: 21%
NPS: 25 (n=90)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 8%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: -6 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 7 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Mina Gupta): "If we cannot show credible progress this quarter, procurement pushes these deals into next fiscal."
VP Engineering (Owen Lopez): "We can move quickly if every blocker has an owner and fallback path."
Head of Customer Success (Anya Rossi): "Customers are asking for predictability in week one more than net-new functionality."
Commercial note: 8 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 48 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Skyline Telecom (Operations Lead): "This solved one team workflow immediately, then stalled when we tried to scale."
Delta Field Services (IT Manager): "Peak-hour reliability issues are why leadership hesitates to expand usage."
Northgate Logistics (Director of Product): "We do not need every advanced control on day one, we need momentum in hour one."
Data gap: no structured interviews from churned accounts in the last 30 days.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 36
Median cycle time: 24 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 53%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 7
Release rollback events last quarter: 3
Average time to resolve escalations: 69 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: State your decision in one sentence, then justify it with evidence and constraints.
submissionFields.label: Decision and Recommendation

submissionFields.hint: Compare at least two viable alternatives and what each sacrifices.
submissionFields.label: Alternatives and Trade-offs

submissionFields.hint: Define the primary segment and who is explicitly deprioritized.
submissionFields.label: Who This Optimizes For

submissionFields.hint: List top risks and concrete guardrails to limit downside.
submissionFields.label: Key Risks and Guardrails

submissionFields.hint: Break down phases, owners, and stop/go checkpoints.
submissionFields.label: 90-Day Execution Plan

rubric.name: Decision Quality
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is the recommendation specific, evidence-based, and timely?

rubric.name: Trade-off Rigor
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are trade-offs explicit, quantified where possible, and defensible?

rubric.name: Stakeholder Awareness
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does the proposal handle competing incentives credibly?

rubric.name: Intellectual Honesty
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are uncertainty and assumptions clearly acknowledged?

expertSolution.content: Recommendation: run a bounded pilot with reliability guardrails. Baseline today is activation 61% and 30-day retention 21% across 37179 active accounts. Scale only if activation improves by at least 4 points without retention regression.

Option A is broad rollout now to support near-term sales urgency tied to 8 late-stage deals. Option B is reliability-only focus with deferred expansion. Option C combines targeted rollout with dependency reduction from 7 unresolved items. Option C is the best risk-adjusted path.

Optimize for repeat-use mid-market teams where onboarding friction is most visible in recent escalations (48). Deprioritize bespoke edge cases this cycle because cycle time is 24 days and on-time delivery is 53%.

Key risks are dependency slippage, overreliance on projected revenue, and trust erosion from reliability events. Guardrails: weekly dependency review with Owen Lopez, explicit non-goals, and rollback if escalation volume increases more than 15% week-over-week.

Weeks 1-2 lock scope and instrumentation. Weeks 3-6 run pilot and reliability fixes. Weeks 7-10 evaluate cohort movement against activation/retention deltas and NPS confidence limits (n=90). Weeks 11-12 decide scale, pivot, or stop with documented trade-offs.
